verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, sperm-surface immunoglobulin-like proteins involved in gamete fusion. IZUMO4 lacks a TM anchor itself but forms a stable complex with other IZUMO members and is displayed on the sperm surface during fertilization — a narrow germline-restricted surface context. Despite the NCBI nuclear annotation, the IZUMO family biology and disulfide-linked complex partnership with TM IZUMO proteins on sperm support contextual surface accessibility restricted to the gamete lineage.
